expectations.text: Stage move identified as going to Signed Approvals specifically
expectations.evidence: Explicitly targets the closest live match ('Approved') while clearly stating the intent is the 'Signed Approvals' stage, and asks Alvin to confirm the naming equivalence.

expectations.text: IL Review Gate side-effect explicitly previewed
expectations.evidence: 'IL Status custom field would flip: null → "Pending IL Review"' shown in the Rule 1 preview.

expectations.text: Regulatory inbound task prefix mentioned in the atomic preview
expectations.evidence: '[IL Review Pending — claims-il-and-label-keeper]' task creation detailed step by step.

expectations.text: Framed as Rule 1 confirmation preview, not executed
expectations.evidence: 'STOPPING HERE, NOT EXECUTED' plus a 'Proceed?' gate.
No write tool calls made; explicitly stated.

eval_feedback.overall: Real finding, not a false positive: the live Asana workspace has no section literally named 'Signed Approvals' — the skill's own reference doc (stage-gate-procedure.md) is stale against the actual project structure. The run correctly refused to fire the IL Review Gate on what might be a no-op move without flagging it first. This is a genuine doc/reality mismatch worth fixing in the skill, independent of the eval.
